verses.text: Then came near the heads of the fathers of the Levites unto Eleazar the priest, and unto Joshua son of Nun, and unto the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel; they spake unto them at Shiloh in the land of Canaan, saying, The LORD commanded by the hand of Moses to give us cities to dwell in, with the suburbs thereof for our cattle. The children of Israel gave unto the Levites out of their inheritance, at the commandment of the LORD, these cities and their suburbs. Guidance: After eleven tribes have settled their territories, the Levites come forward — last. They cite scripture: 'The LORD commanded by Moses.' They are not begging; they are reminding. Numbers 35:1-8 already provided for them. Faith for the tribe without land is faith in the word, not in the lot. The chapter opens with a humble request based not on entitlement but on a remembered promise.

Then came near the heads of the fathers of the Levites unto Eleazar the priest, and unto Joshua son of Nun, and unto the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel; they spake unto them at Shiloh in the land of Canaan, saying, The LORD commanded by the hand of Moses to give us cities to dwell in, with the suburbs thereof for our cattle. The children of Israel gave unto the Levites out of their inheritance, at the commandment of the LORD, these cities and their suburbs. Guidance: After eleven tribes have settled their territories, the Levites come forward — last. They cite scripture: 'The LORD commanded by Moses.' They are not begging; they are reminding. Numbers 35:1-8 already provided for them. Faith for the tribe without land is faith in the word, not in the lot. The chapter opens with a humble request based not on entitlement but on a remembered promise.

The lot came out for the families of the Kohathites: the children of Aaron the priest, who were of the Levites, had by lot out of the tribe of Judah, of the tribe of Simeon, and out of the tribe of Benjamin, thirteen cities. The rest of the children of Kohath had by lot out of the families of the tribe of Ephraim, Dan, and out of the half tribe of Manasseh, ten cities. The children of Gershon had by lot out of the families of the tribe of Issachar, Asher, Naphtali, and out of the half tribe of Manasseh in Bashan, thirteen cities. The children of Merari by their families had out of the tribe of Reuben, Gad, and Zebulun, twelve cities. The children of Israel gave by lot unto the Levites these cities with their suburbs, as the LORD commanded by the hand of Moses. Guidance: Forty-eight cities, scattered across all twelve tribes' territories. The priest-line (Aaron's sons within Kohath) gets cities in Judah, Simeon, Benjamin — near where the temple will one day stand in Jerusalem. The other Levite families spread out into the rest. No tribe is more than walking distance from a Levite. God refuses to ghetto His ministers: priests live among every people, in every region. The map of inheritance is the map of pastoral care.

They gave out of the tribe of the children of Judah, and out of the tribe of the children of Simeon, these cities which are here mentioned by name, which the children of Aaron, being of the families of the Kohathites who were of the children of Levi, had: for theirs was the first lot. They gave them the city of Arba the father of Anak, which is Hebron, in the hill country of Judah, with the suburbs thereof round about it. The fields of the city, and the villages thereof, gave they to Caleb son of Jephunneh for his possession. Thus unto the children of Aaron the priest they gave Hebron with her suburbs, to be a city of refuge for the slayer; and Libnah with her suburbs, Jattir with her suburbs, Eshtemoa with her suburbs, Holon with her suburbs, Debir with her suburbs, Ain with her suburbs, Juttah with her suburbs, Beth-shemesh with her suburbs — nine cities out of those two tribes. Out of the tribe of Benjamin: Gibeon with her suburbs, Geba with her suburbs, Anathoth with her suburbs, Almon with her suburbs — four cities. All the cities of the children of Aaron, the priests, were thirteen cities with their suburbs. Guidance: The priest-line gets thirteen cities, including Hebron (giant-slayer Caleb's portion!) — and the text takes care: Caleb keeps the farmland; the priests get the city. The two grace generations divide what could have been a quarrel. Anathoth is on the list — that will be the prophet Jeremiah's hometown six centuries later. The seeds of the prophetic future are sown in priest-city assignments. God plants prophets where He plants altars.

The families of the children of Kohath, the Levites which remained of the children of Kohath, even they had the cities of their lot out of the tribe of Ephraim. They gave them Shechem with her suburbs in mount Ephraim, to be a city of refuge for the slayer; Gezer with her suburbs, Kibzaim with her suburbs, Beth-horon with her suburbs — four cities. Out of the tribe of Dan: Eltekeh with her suburbs, Gibbethon with her suburbs, Aijalon with her suburbs, Gath-rimmon with her suburbs — four cities. Out of the half tribe of Manasseh: Tanach with her suburbs, Gath-rimmon with her suburbs — two cities. All the cities were ten with their suburbs for the families of the children of Kohath that remained. Guidance: The rest of the Kohathites get ten cities — among them Shechem, where Abram first heard 'I will give this land' (Gen 12:7) and where Joseph's bones will eventually rest. The deepest covenant memory site is held by Levite hands, lest Israel forget. Even the most spiritually charged ground is staffed with caretakers. Important geography needs faithful occupants.

Guidance: The rest of the Kohathites get ten cities — among them Shechem, where Abram first heard 'I will give this land' (Gen 12:7) and where Joseph's bones will eventually rest. The deepest covenant memory site is held by Levite hands, lest Israel forget. Even the most spiritually charged ground is staffed with caretakers. Important geography needs faithful occupants.

The families of the children of Kohath, the Levites which remained of the children of Kohath, even they had the cities of their lot out of the tribe of Ephraim. They gave them Shechem with her suburbs in mount Ephraim, to be a city of refuge for the slayer; Gezer with her suburbs, Kibzaim with her suburbs, Beth-horon with her suburbs — four cities. Out of the tribe of Dan: Eltekeh with her suburbs, Gibbethon with her suburbs, Aijalon with her suburbs, Gath-rimmon with her suburbs — four cities. Out of the half tribe of Manasseh: Tanach with her suburbs, Gath-rimmon with her suburbs — two cities. All the cities were ten with their suburbs for the families of the children of Kohath that remained. Guidance: The rest of the Kohathites get ten cities — among them Shechem, where Abram first heard 'I will give this land' (Gen 12:7) and where Joseph's bones will eventually rest. The deepest covenant memory site is held by Levite hands, lest Israel forget. Even the most spiritually charged ground is staffed with caretakers. Important geography needs faithful occupants.

Unto the children of Gershon, of the families of the Levites, out of the other half tribe of Manasseh they gave Golan in Bashan with her suburbs, to be a city of refuge for the slayer; and Be-eshterah with her suburbs — two cities. Out of the tribe of Issachar: Kishon with her suburbs, Dabareh with her suburbs, Jarmuth with her suburbs, En-gannim with her suburbs — four cities. Out of the tribe of Asher: Mishal with her suburbs, Abdon with her suburbs, Helkath with her suburbs, Rehob with her suburbs — four cities. Out of the tribe of Naphtali: Kedesh in Galilee with her suburbs, to be a city of refuge for the slayer; Hammoth-dor with her suburbs, Kartan with her suburbs — three cities. All the cities of the Gershonites according to their families were thirteen cities with their suburbs. Guidance: Gershon, the eldest line of Levi, gets thirteen cities in the north — Golan and Kedesh among them, the two northern cities of refuge. The eldest line is not given the priesthood (that went to Aaron's Kohathite branch) but they get the territory closest to the borders, where Israel meets pagan neighbors. The first contact zone gets the senior caretakers. God places His most experienced ministers nearest the cultural edge.

Unto the families of the children of Merari, the rest of the Levites, out of the tribe of Zebulun: Jokneam with her suburbs, Kartah with her suburbs, Dimnah with her suburbs, Nahalal with her suburbs — four cities. Out of the tribe of Reuben: Bezer with her suburbs, to be a city of refuge for the slayer; Jahazah with her suburbs, Kedemoth with her suburbs, Mephaath with her suburbs — four cities. Out of the tribe of Gad: Ramoth in Gilead with her suburbs, to be a city of refuge for the slayer; Mahanaim with her suburbs, Heshbon with her suburbs, Jazer with her suburbs — four cities in all. So all the cities for the children of Merari by their families, which were remaining of the families of the Levites, were by their lot twelve cities. Guidance: Merari gets twelve cities, including the two east-of-Jordan refuges (Bezer, Ramoth-Gilead). Mahanaim — where Jacob saw the angels — is on Merari's list. Heshbon — where King Sihon once ruled — is too. The same geography that once hosted pagan kings now hosts Levitical singers and porters. The redemption of place is part of the conquest.

All the cities of the Levites within the possession of the children of Israel were forty and eight cities with their suburbs. These cities were every one with their suburbs round about them: thus were all these cities. Guidance: Forty-eight cities. Twelve tribes, one tithe — each tribe gives roughly four. The math itself is worship. Levites cannot withdraw to a religious quarter; they are baked into every region's life. The 'suburbs' (pasture rings) are part of every grant — God's ministers are housed but also feed their flocks. Spiritual work and ordinary work share a fence-line.

All the cities of the Levites within the possession of the children of Israel were forty and eight cities with their suburbs. These cities were every one with their suburbs round about them: thus were all these cities. Guidance: Forty-eight cities. Twelve tribes, one tithe — each tribe gives roughly four. The math itself is worship. Levites cannot withdraw to a religious quarter; they are baked into every region's life. The 'suburbs' (pasture rings) are part of every grant — God's ministers are housed but also feed their flocks. Spiritual work and ordinary work share a fence-line.

The LORD gave unto Israel all the land which he sware to give unto their fathers; they possessed it, and dwelt therein. The LORD gave them rest round about, according to all that he sware unto their fathers: there stood not a man of all their enemies before them; the LORD delivered all their enemies into their hand. There failed not ought of any good thing which the LORD had spoken unto the house of Israel; all came to pass. Guidance: Three sentences that summarize the whole book. Every clause is in past tense: gave, possessed, dwelt, gave rest, delivered. And the climactic line: 'There failed not ought of any good thing which the LORD had spoken; all came to pass.' Centuries of promise — to Abraham, to Moses, to Joshua — accounted for, paid in full, ledger balanced. Israel is not yet fully obedient (Geshurites, Maacathites, Jebusites still linger), but God is fully faithful. The book frames the difference: human compromise does not cancel divine completion.
